Motion To Specifically Enforce The Parties Settlement Agreement And To Turn Over Funds
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nathan Metcalf, esq. (415-777-3200) of Hanson Bridgett for Plaintiffs.
Stephen A. McKae, esq. (510-834=6600) of Wendel Rosen for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ALICE BOTTINO, ECHCO SALES CO A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, and JOAN FOSTER's Motion To Specifically Enforce The Parties' Settlement Agreement And To Turn Over Funds.

Plaintiffs and Cross-Defendants Alice Bottino, as trustee of the Alice Bottino Trust, and Echco Sales Company's motion to specifically enforce the parties' settlement agreement and to turn over funds is granted. The Settlement Agreement and Release provides that the full amount of the funds in the Blocked Account, with interest, shall be released to the Trust if, among other things, Site Closure is not obtained by April 13, 2014, a deadline that was later extended by the Court to April 13, 2016. It is undisputed that Defendant did not obtain Site Closure by the court-ordered deadline of April 13, 2016, and has not sought a further extension in the intervening three-plus years. This breach of Defendant's obligations under the Settlement Agreement support exercise of the Court's reserved jurisdiction under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 to enter judgment on the agreement and to order the return to Plaintiffs of the funds in the Blocked Account. Defendant's arguments that the Settlement Agreement may not be specifically enforced under Civil Code Sections 3390 and 3391 are unpersuasive. Defendants' proposal to file a belated motion for a further extension of the Site Closure deadline would not cure those breaches. In any event, it is unclear that a further extension could produce a final resolution of this dated case within any reasonable period of time, given Defendants' consultant's prediction that site closure is unlikely to be achieved in less than an additional five years. Plaintiffs' motion for an award of attorneys' fees is granted in the amount of $51,156.00, plus $250.90 in costs. (Metcalf Decl. Par. 9.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
